auditor.summary: The generated code implements the core mobile navigation infrastructure correctly — MobileNav component with hamburger button (48×48px), slide-over drawer with backdrop and Escape key handling, and Sidebar hidden on mobile via responsive classes. DashboardShell properly integrates both components with responsive margin classes. However, two critical files specified in the build spec are missing from the generated output: KpiGrid.tsx (grid column responsiveness) and globals.css (font-size enforcement, table overflow, touch target CSS). These omissions mean 4 of the 8 acceptance criteria are only partially satisfied. The build is recoverable with the addition of these two files. Score is 72 due to missing critical CSS and component modifications that are essential for full acceptance criteria compliance.

top_issues.description: KpiGrid.tsx modification is specified in the build spec but the generated code does not include this file. The grid column classes need to be changed to 'grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3 md:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-6' for responsive behavior.

globals.css modification is specified in the build spec but the generated code does not include this file. Three critical CSS rules are missing: (1) body font-size enforcement via max(14px, 1rem), (2) .table-responsive class with overflow-x: auto, (3) a/button touch target min-height/min-width at mobile breakpoint.

MobileNav.tsx duplicates the navigation items list from Sidebar.tsx. This creates a maintenance burden — any nav item added/removed must be updated in both files. The build spec says to 'contain the existing Sidebar nav tree' but this implementation duplicates it.
